pages.raw_text:
Meeting for last love feast and
shower of verbal bouquets for the
good work of its various members,
the executive committee for the
Spokane county display at the
Yukon Pacific exposition,
heard the final report of it's secre-
tary, in the gothic room of the
Davenport restaurant last night.
With all bills paid, there is, a bal
ance of $1030S0 still in the treas
ury, out of the total receipts of
$31 ,016.90 from the county and from
popular subscriptions.


All members of the committee
were profuse in their praise of Mr..
and Mrs. Martin j. Vessels, the
famous local decorators, who super
intended the arrangement and dec
Grafton of the beautiful Spokane
county building, erected after the
plans of julius little of the archi-
tectural firm of Preusse & Sittle.


1t was shown that, with one pos
sible exception, the Spokane county
was the most attractive
and impressive on the grounds, and,
as a token of eheir appreciation,
the committee presented Mrs. Wes-
sells with hondsome gold watch.


Following is list of the disburse-
ments of the committee as reported
by Secretary Monroe:


Total cost of display, $29.9S6;
construction of building, S9534 so;
assembling exhibit, $4,000: mainten-
ance, $780, salaries, $4,000, and
lighting, s214777.


Those present at the last sup
ner' were i. MacLean, E. F. C. Van
Dissel. L. F. Williams, r. A. Jones,
Sam Glasgow. J. A. Ristau. r. A.
Hutchinson, j. A. Zittel, H. w. Col
Inns, E. D. Sanders, H. J. Shinn and
Gus Meese Of the committee and
II. L. Blocker. c. M. Fassett, M. J.
Vessels, L. G. Monroe, A. w. Jones
and w. S. McCrea.
